summary: Hidden payload loader wired into module import path
details: index.js requires './lib/trigger' alongside an author comment explicitly describing it as a disguised payload trigger ('Import trigger to enable payload (looks like normal initialization)'). The package.json declares files ['index.js','lib/'] and index.js also requires './lib/formatter' and './lib/validator', but no lib/ directory is shipped in the tarball, so require() throws at load time and the package is non-functional as a JSON formatter. The advertised 'JSON formatting/validation' purpose is a cover story; the import path is structured around loading a hidden trigger module at require()-time. Even though the lib/ payload is absent in this tarball (making this version a staging artifact rather than a working dropper), the authored intent and wiring are a backdoor shape that should not reach installers — a future republish or sibling version supplying lib/trigger would execute attacker-controlled code on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
